title: 3 Growth Stocks With Strong Insider Confidence
content:
The United States market has experienced a positive trajectory, rising 1.8% over the last week and climbing 28% in the past year, with earnings projected to grow by 17% annually. In this favorable environment, growth stocks with strong insider ownership can be particularly attractive as they often indicate confidence from those closest to the company's operations and potential.

Top 10 Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In The United States

Name Insider Ownership Earnings Growth Uxin (UXIN) 34.5% 74.1% Upstart Holdings (UPST) 14.1% 58.1% QT Imaging Holdings (QTI) 23.9% 104.2% Karman Holdings (KRMN) 15.6% 52.6% FirstSun Capital Bancorp (FSUN) 21% 54.2% EHang Holdings (EH) 29.6% 55.4% Corcept Therapeutics (CORT) 11.7% 48.9% Astera Labs (ALAB) 10.1% 31.5% AppLovin (APP) 27.4% 21.7% Abeona Therapeutics (ABEO) 16.7% 32.9%

Click here to see the full list of 174 stocks from our Fast Growing US Companies With High Insider Ownership screener.

Here's a peek at a few of the choices from the screener.

Intapp

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Intapp, Inc., via its subsidiary Integration Appliance, Inc., offers AI-powered solutions globally, with a market cap of approximately $1.59 billion.

Operations: Intapp generates revenue primarily from its Software & Programming segment, which accounts for $560.31 million.

Insider Ownership: 11%

Intapp is positioned as a growth company with significant insider ownership, leveraging its advanced AI-driven solutions like DealCloud and Intapp Time to enhance client relationship management and operational efficiency. Recent partnerships with firms such as Ropes & Gray and Arkwright Consulting underscore its strategic expansion in professional services. Despite reporting a net loss, Intapp's revenue is growing faster than the US market average, with forecasts indicating profitability within three years. The stock trades at a substantial discount relative to its estimated fair value.

Delve into the full analysis future growth report here for a deeper understanding of Intapp. Our expertly prepared valuation report Intapp implies its share price may be lower than expected.INTA Ownership Breakdown as at May 2026

Uxin

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Uxin Limited, along with its subsidiaries, operates as a used car retailer in the People's Republic of China and has a market cap of $512.55 million.

Operations: The company's revenue primarily comes from its Internet Information Providers segment, which generated CN¥3.24 billion.

Insider Ownership: 34.5%

Uxin is expanding its used car superstore network in China, with recent openings in Tianjin and plans for additional locations. The company forecasts revenue growth of 36% annually, outpacing the US market. Despite auditor concerns over its financial stability and a net loss reported for 2025, Uxin's strategic partnerships aim to bolster its position in the automotive sector. Insider ownership remains high, with no significant insider trading activity noted recently.

Story Continues

Click to explore a detailed breakdown of our findings in Uxin's earnings growth report. In light of our recent valuation report, it seems possible that Uxin is trading beyond its estimated value.UXIN Earnings and Revenue Growth as at May 2026

JinkoSolar Holding

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: JinkoSolar Holding Co., Ltd. and its subsidiaries are involved in the design, development, production, and marketing of photovoltaic products with a market cap of approximately $1.19 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue primarily through its manufacturing segment, which amounted to CN¥63.90 billion.

Insider Ownership: 36.5%

JinkoSolar, a significant player in the solar industry, has high insider ownership and is expected to achieve profitability within three years, outpacing average market growth. Despite recent financial challenges with a net loss reported for Q1 2026, revenue is forecasted to grow at 14.8% annually. The company recently signed a major agreement with PM Green for up to 1 GW of capacity and continues innovating with advanced photovoltaic technologies like its Tiger Neo modules.

title: QT Imaging outlines ~$39M 2026 revenue outlook as AMA CPT code takes effect January 1, 2027
content:
Earnings Call Insights: QT Imaging Holdings (QTI) Q1 2026

MANAGEMENT VIEW

 	* "The first quarter of 2026 was a highly productive period for QT Imaging as we continue to execute across commercialization, clinical validation, reimbursement, regulatory expansion and software innovation." (CEO & Director Raluca Dinu)
 	* "During the quarter, we shipped 13 QT Breast Acoustic CT systems, which was in line with our commercial distribution commitments." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "Subsequent to the close of the quarter, the maturity date of our senior secured term loan with Lynrock Lake was extended by 2 years from March 31, 2027, to March 31, 2029." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "We achieved an important milestone with the approval by the American Medical Association of a new Category III CPT code for our technology, effective January 1, 2027." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "We also received FDA 510(k) clearance for enhancements to our Breast Acoustic CT scanner and platform designed to improve visualization and imaging coverage of posterior breast tissue." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "During the quarter, we launched the QT Imaging-Olea Viewer" and "we released software version 4.5.0, a next-generation image reconstruction update that improves spatial resolution and reflection imaging." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "Revenue for the first quarter of 2026 was $6.5 million" and "as of March 31, 2026, the company had cash, restricted cash and cash equivalents of $7 million." (Chief Financial Officer Jay Jennings)

OUTLOOK

 	* "We are affirming our expectation for revenue of approximately $39 million for the year" and "this guidance is based on the expected shipment of scanners under distributor minimum order quantities as well as the early monetization of cloud-based services as we bring our platform online across clinical and research customers." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Under our distribution agreements, in addition to the 60 scanners contracted by NXC Imaging for 2026, Gulf Medical is contracted for 20 scanners in 2026" and "Al Naghi Medical is contracted for 7 scanners in 2026." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "We are discussing with NXC Imaging and Canon USA further business beyond 2026." (CEO & Director Dinu)

FINANCIAL RESULTS

 	* "Revenue for the first quarter of 2026 was $6.5 million" and "the increase was primarily attributable to the shipment of 13 Breast Acoustic CT scanners during the quarter." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Cost of revenue for Q1 2026 was $3.9 million" and "gross margin for the 2026 quarter" was "41%." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Total operating expenses for the first quarter of 2026 were $5 million" and "net loss for the first quarter of 2026 was $3.4 million." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "EBITDA for Q1 2026 was negative $2.5 million" and "adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2026 was negative $1.5 million." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Net cash used in operating activities during the first quarter of 2026 was $3.7 million." (CFO Jennings)
 	* "We amended our credit agreement with Lynrock Lake, extending the maturity date of the $10.1 million senior secured term loan to March 31, 2029, and increasing the interest rate from 10% per annum to 12% per annum." (CFO Jennings)

Q&A

 	* Jeffrey Cohen, Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc., Research Division: "What's the regulatory status in Saudi? Is it approved?" CEO Dinu: "Saudi Arabia FDA approval is still to be received" and "we do hope within the next month or so, we're going to get our SFDA approval." 
 	* Cohen, Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc., Research Division: "Would you anticipate Saudi approval this calendar year?" CEO Dinu: "Saudi Arabia FDA, Jeff, it should be within a month or so." 
 	* Cohen, Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc., Research Division: "I wanted to jump over to your Canon agreement" and "tell us if there's been any updates recently as far as time line" CEO Dinu: "the contract is for 2026" and "we are discussing with NXC Imaging and Canon USA further business beyond 2026."
 	* Benjamin Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: "are there any sort of edge cases, whether it's like calcification" CEO Dinu: "calcification is the one finding critical for -- that gets discovered via our reflection" and "The 4.5.0 software version directly improves resolution for us to get calcification." 
 	* Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: "Any more to share" on "longitudinal imaging/biopharma" CEO Dinu: "we are ready to submit a manuscript for a paper with clear results that show the nonresponders of various therapies" and "the volume is clearly showing the increase or decrease with much higher precision." 
 	* Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: "Are there other discussions that are ongoing" for "new distribution agreements" CEO Dinu: "we have other 4 countries in the Gulf region we're trying to cover" and "we'll work through the year to get our paperwork together for CE Mark" and "We'll probably have that up and running Q2 next year." 

SENTIMENT ANALYSIS

 	* Analysts: slightly positive, with questions centered on commercialization timing and international regulatory gating, including "What's the regulatory status in Saudi? Is it approved?" (Cohen) and cadence/expansion prompts such as "Are there other discussions that are ongoing" (Haynor).
 	* Management: positive and forward-leaning in prepared remarks ("we remain focused on disciplined execution") and confident but conditional in Q&A on timing and geopolitics ("we do hope within the next month or so" and "As soon as the war gives us a little bit of a break, we'll start shipping in UAE"). (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* Versus last quarter: the prepared-message tone stayed opportunity-focused while Q&A shifted toward near-term execution details (Saudi/UAE timing, NXC/Canon beyond 2026), compared with the prior call’s broader platform narrative and multi-item 2026 news flow framing.

QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER COMPARISON

 	* Guidance language was unchanged, with CFO Jennings repeating "revenue of approximately $39 million for the year" in both calls.
 	* The current call added financing specifics post-quarter close, including the interest-rate change, stating the loan extension also included "increasing the interest rate from 10% per annum to 12% per annum" (CFO Jennings), while the prior call discussed balance sheet strength and prior financing activities.
 	* The current call emphasized in-quarter commercialization execution ("shipped 13 QT Breast Acoustic CT systems") (CEO & Director Dinu), while the prior call emphasized full-year 2025 scale-up ("we shipped 40 scanners") and Q4 shipment volume.
 	* Analyst focus broadened from utilization ramps and future news flow in the prior call to near-term regulatory clearance timing (SFDA) and distribution continuity beyond 2026 in the current call.

RISKS AND CONCERNS

 	* "Saudi Arabia FDA approval is still to be received" and "As soon as the war gives us a little bit of a break, we'll start shipping in UAE." (CEO & Director Dinu)
 	* "increasing the interest rate from 10% per annum to 12% per annum" on the Lynrock Lake term loan amendment. (CFO Jennings)
 	* "Total operating expenses" increased year-over-year, with management attributing the rise primarily to "higher employee compensation and professional and outside service costs." (CFO Jennings)

FINAL TAKEAWAY

QT Imaging management highlighted Q1 shipment execution (13 systems), reiterated full-year 2026 revenue guidance of approximately $39 million tied to distributor minimum order quantities and early cloud monetization, and pointed to reimbursement and regulatory milestones, including an AMA Category III CPT code effective January 1, 2027 and FDA 510(k) clearance for posterior tissue coverage enhancements. In Q&A, management framed Saudi regulatory clearance as near-term ("within a month or so"), described geopolitical timing constraints on UAE shipping, and said it is in discussions with NXC Imaging and Canon USA regarding business beyond the 2026 contract.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/qti/earnings/transcripts]

MORE ON QT IMAGING HOLDINGS

 	* QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. (QTI) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4904304-qt-imaging-holdings-inc-qti-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. 2026 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4904207-qt-imaging-holdings-inc-2026-q1-results-earnings-call-presentation]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings, Inc. (QTI) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4888126-qt-imaging-holdings-inc-qti-q4-2025-earnings-call-transcript]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings GAAP EPS of -$0.10, revenue of $8.28M; reaffirms FY26 outlook [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4568435-qt-imaging-holdings-gaap-eps-of-010-revenue-of-828m-reaffirms-fy26-outlook]
 	* QT Imaging Holdings Preliminary GAAP EPS of -$0.10, revenue of $8.3M [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4553555-qt-imaging-holdings-gaap-eps-of-0_10-revenue-of-8_3m]

title: Clover Health Investments And 2 Other Insider Picks For Growth
content:
Over the last 7 days, the United States market has risen by 2.2%, contributing to a substantial 31% climb over the past year, with earnings expected to grow by 17% annually. In such an environment, stocks with high insider ownership can be appealing as they often indicate confidence from those closest to the company’s operations and potential for growth, making them noteworthy considerations for investors seeking opportunities in this thriving market.

Top 10 Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In The United States

Here we highlight a subset of our preferred stocks from the screener.

Clover Health Investments

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Clover Health Investments Corp. offers Medicare Advantage plans in the United States and has a market cap of $1.64 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue primarily comes from its insurance segment, which generated $2.21 billion.

Insider Ownership: 22.1%

Clover Health Investments is experiencing strong growth, with revenue rising to US$749.19 million in Q1 2026 from US$462.33 million a year ago and achieving net income of US$27.33 million. Despite significant insider selling recently, the company trades at a good value relative to peers and industry standards, being 90.4% below its estimated fair value. Revenue is expected to grow faster than the broader market at 14.8% annually, though insider buying has not been substantial recently.

Take a closer look at Clover Health Investments' potential here in our earnings growth report. Upon reviewing our latest valuation report, Clover Health Investments' share price might be too pessimistic.CLOV Earnings and Revenue Growth as at May 2026

ATRenew

Overview: ATRenew Inc. operates by selling pre-owned consumer electronics through online platforms and other channels in China, with a market cap of approximately $1.05 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily generated from its retail electronics segment, which amounted to CN¥21.05 billion.

Insider Ownership: 11.9%

ATRenew's recent performance is marked by a significant turnaround, achieving net income of CNY 336.29 million for 2025 from a previous loss. The company forecasts revenue growth between 25.9% and 28.1% year-over-year for Q1 2026, indicating robust momentum despite slower annual revenue growth projections of 19.2%. Earnings are expected to grow significantly at over 35% annually, outpacing the US market average. Insider ownership remains strong with no substantial insider trading activity recently reported.

Ryan Specialty Holdings

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Ryan Specialty Holdings, Inc. provides specialty products and solutions for insurance brokers, agents, and carriers across various regions globally, with a market cap of $8.26 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue of $3.10 billion from its Insurance Brokers segment, providing specialized services for brokers, agents, and carriers across multiple regions.

Insider Ownership: 12.1%

Ryan Specialty Holdings showcases strong growth potential with earnings projected to rise significantly at 46.9% annually, outpacing the US market. Recent results highlight a turnaround from a net loss to a net income of US$40.6 million in Q1 2026, alongside substantial insider buying activity. Despite slower revenue growth forecasts of 7.8%, the company remains undervalued and actively engages in share buybacks and strategic acquisitions, reinforcing its commitment to shareholder value enhancement.

title: May 2026's Leading Growth Stocks With Insider Influence
content:
Over the last 7 days, the United States market has risen by 2.2%, contributing to a robust 31% increase over the past year, with earnings projected to grow by 17% annually. In this thriving environment, growth companies with high insider ownership are particularly noteworthy as they often align management's interests with those of shareholders, potentially driving long-term performance.

Top 10 Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership In The United States

Name Insider Ownership Earnings Growth Uxin (UXIN) 35.7% 74.1% Upstart Holdings (UPST) 12.8% 58.5% QT Imaging Holdings (QTI) 26.3% 110.2% Precigen (PGEN) 11.9% 68.4% Karman Holdings (KRMN) 17% 53.2% Corcept Therapeutics (CORT) 11.8% 48.7% Clene (CLNN) 10.9% 62.2% Caledonia Mining (CMCL) 14.1% 29.6% Astera Labs (ALAB) 10.8% 31.5% AppLovin (APP) 27.4% 21.4%

Click here to see the full list of 188 stocks from our Fast Growing US Companies With High Insider Ownership screener.

We'll examine a selection from our screener results.

Himax Technologies

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Himax Technologies, Inc. is a fabless semiconductor company that offers display imaging processing technologies across various countries including China, Taiwan, Korea, Japan, the United States, and Mexico, with a market cap of $3.10 billion.

Operations: Himax Technologies generates revenue through its provision of display imaging processing technologies across multiple international markets, including China, Taiwan, Korea, Japan, the United States, and Mexico.

Insider Ownership: 29.2%

Earnings Growth Forecast: 65.7% p.a.

Himax Technologies is experiencing significant earnings growth, forecasted at 65.67% annually, surpassing the US market's average. However, its profit margins have declined from 9.5% to 3.9% year-over-year, and revenue has decreased to US$199 million in Q1 2026 from US$215 million a year ago. Despite this, upcoming projects and innovative product advancements in microdisplay technology for AR glasses suggest potential future growth opportunities amidst current financial challenges.

Delve into the full analysis future growth report here for a deeper understanding of Himax Technologies. Insights from our recent valuation report point to the potential overvaluation of Himax Technologies shares in the market.HIMX Ownership Breakdown as at May 2026

On Holding

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: On Holding AG develops and distributes performance sports products under the On brand across various global regions, with a market cap of approximately $11.66 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue primarily comes from the Athletic Footwear segment, generating CHF 3.01 billion.

Story Continues

Insider Ownership: 25.9%

Earnings Growth Forecast: 24.4% p.a.

On Holding is undergoing significant organizational changes to align its founder-led vision with execution, promoting Scott Maguire as President & COO and appointing co-founders David Allemann and Caspar Coppetti as Co-CEOs. Despite a decline in profit margins from 10.5% to 6.8%, the company's earnings are forecasted to grow significantly at 24.38% annually, outpacing the US market's average growth rate. Revenue guidance for 2026 indicates an expected increase of at least 23%, reaching CHF 3.44 billion on a constant currency basis, reflecting robust growth potential amidst strategic leadership transitions.

Navigate through the intricacies of On Holding with our comprehensive analyst estimates report here. In light of our recent valuation report, it seems possible that On Holding is trading beyond its estimated value.ONON Earnings and Revenue Growth as at May 2026

Warby Parker

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Warby Parker Inc. operates as a retailer of eyewear products through its physical stores and online platform in the United States and Canada, with a market cap of approximately $3.63 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily derived from its Holistic Vision Care segment, amounting to $890.57 million.

Insider Ownership: 14.9%

Earnings Growth Forecast: 71.2% p.a.

Warby Parker's earnings are expected to grow significantly at 71.2% annually, surpassing the US market's average growth rate of 16.7%, despite a low forecasted Return on Equity of 17.5%. The company reported Q1 sales of US$242.45 million, slightly up from the previous year, with net income marginally decreasing to US$3.18 million. Recent product expansions into performance eyewear and a share repurchase program signal strategic initiatives to bolster growth and shareholder value.

Unlock comprehensive insights into our analysis of Warby Parker stock in this growth report. The valuation report we've compiled suggests that Warby Parker's current price could be inflated.WRBY Ownership Breakdown as at May 2026

Find the next big opportunity with these 14 smaller AI-focused companies with strong growth potential through early-stage innovation in machine learning, automation, and data intelligence that could fund your retirement.
